summary: Linked the itlucky item data by adding two ItemStateTable arrays, it_803F8190 and it_803F8200, to src/melee/it/items/itlucky.c. The arrays wire existing animation, physics, and collision callback symbols into the item state table layout. decomp.dev reported one new GALE01 match: main/melee/it/items/itlucky .data improved from 0.00% to 100.00%, adding +192 matched bytes and +0.02% matched data overall.
what_changed: Added ItemStateTable it_803F8190[] with seven entries before it_802D5050 in itlucky.c.
Added ItemStateTable it_803F8200[] with five entries before it_802D5050 in itlucky.c.

The new table entries reference existing callbacks including it_802D55DC, it_802D5600, it_802D5620, itLucky_UnkMotion5_Anim, itLucky_UnkMotion5_Phys, itLucky_UnkMotion5_Coll, it_802D53AC, it_802D53F0, it_802D53F4, itLucky_UnkMotion6_Anim, itLucky_UnkMotion6_Phys, it_802D5884, it_802D58BC, it_802D58C0, itLucky_UnkMotion3_Anim, itLucky_UnkMotion3_Phys, itLucky_UnkMotion3_Coll, itLucky_UnkMotion2_Anim, itLucky_UnkMotion2_Phys, it_802D5A2C, it_802D5A64, and it_802D5A68.
Some state table collision callback slots are explicitly NULL, matching the expected data layout.
No function bodies were changed in the diff excerpt; the PR was focused on linking static item state table data.
smart_moves: Focused on the exact .data gap for main/melee/it/items/itlucky rather than changing code, producing a clean data-only match.
Used the existing ItemStateTable struct layout directly, preserving the conventional callback order of motion/state ID, animation callback, physics callback, and collision callback.
Kept unresolved or not-yet-meaningfully-named data symbols as address-style names such as it_803F8190 and it_803F8200, avoiding speculative semantic names.
Reused already-decompiled or already-declared callback symbols, minimizing churn and making the table linkable without inventing new behavior.
Represented absent collision callbacks with NULL, which is important for reproducing pointer-table bytes exactly.
decomp_lessons: For item .data matches, reconstructing ItemStateTable arrays can link an entire unit’s data without touching executable code.
State tables often contain repeated callback triplets; preserving repetition exactly is part of the match, not a cleanup opportunity.
When semantic meaning is still unclear, address-based symbols and UnkMotion names are acceptable placeholders that keep progress moving while avoiding false documentation.
The placement and ordering of static data declarations in the C file can matter for linking the expected .data layout.
Bot match reports are useful evidence for validating data reconstruction: this PR moved itlucky .data from 0.00% to 100.00%.
naming_conventions: Anonymous or not-yet-semantic data symbols use address-style names: it_803F8190 and it_803F8200.
Unknown Lucky-specific motion callbacks use the pattern itLucky_UnkMotionN_Anim, itLucky_UnkMotionN_Phys, and itLucky_UnkMotionN_Coll.
Unidentified functions continue to use address-style names such as it_802D55DC and it_802D5A68.
Item state table data is typed as ItemStateTable rather than as raw words or void pointers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Declare concrete ItemStateTable arrays to materialize the expected .data pointer table bytes.
Preserve exact entry order and exact first-field values, including -1, 0, 1, 2, and 3.
Use NULL in callback slots where the original table has no function pointer.
Reuse repeated callback groups verbatim across multiple state entries to match the original data.
Validate the data-only match through decomp.dev’s GALE01 report showing +192 bytes and 100.00% .data for main/melee/it/items/itlucky.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
Automated decomp.dev report confirmed one new match: main/melee/it/items/itlucky .data +192 bytes, 0.00% before to 100.00% after.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename it_803F8190 or it_803F8200 to semantic Lucky item state table names.
Search for later cleanup of itLucky_UnkMotion2, itLucky_UnkMotion3, itLucky_UnkMotion5, and itLucky_UnkMotion6 callback names.
Compare other item files that link ItemStateTable arrays to confirm table naming and placement conventions.
Check headers for extern declarations of it_803F8190 and it_803F8200 if later files reference these tables.
